provenance.title: AGENTICAITA: A Proof-of-Concept About Deliberative Multi-Agent Reasoning for Autonomous Trading Systems
affiliations: Department of Information Engineering, Computer Science, and Mathematics, Center of Excellence for Research DEWS, University of L'Aquila, Italy
provenance.abstract: AGENTICAITA is an agentic AI framework replacing the signal-then-execute paradigm with a fully autonomous deliberative loop using multiple specialized LLM agents that reason, negotiate, and act without offline training or human intervention. Four contributions: AZTE, SDP, IGP, CBD.
key_claims: Training-free, deterministic safety-constrained multi-agent orchestration is feasible in financial decision loops
Sequential Deliberative Pipeline (SDP) with Analyst → Risk Manager → Executor achieves genuine inter-agent negotiation (11.5% agentic friction rate)
AZTE provides regime-invariant cognitive resource allocation via rolling Z-score anomaly detection
CBD composite score enables portfolio-level diversification incentive within individual agent reasoning
System achieved 14.94pp alpha over BTC buy-and-hold during a 5-day dry-run with 157 zero-intervention invocations

modules.name: AZTE
modules.input: raw OHLCV price stream per asset (60s polling)
modules.output: binary trigger signal T_t per asset
modules.operation: rolling Z-score anomaly detection + absolute return floor

modules.name: IGP
modules.output: serialized single pipeline admission or pipeline_busy discard
modules.operation: binary semaphore L; admit iff L=0, set L=1 on entry, L=0 on exit

modules.name: Analyst
modules.input: 20-bar 1-min OHLCV, L2 orderbook, funding rate, market snapshot, CBD score Omega_t, episodic memory briefing
modules.output: JSON: {signal: long|short|wait, confidence: float[0,1], entry_price, stop_loss, take_profit, size_usd, reasoning: string}
modules.operation: zero-shot LLM reasoning (qwen3.5:9b) with structured output prompt

modules.name: RiskManager
modules.output: JSON: {approved: bool, size_usd: float, negotiation_summary: string}
modules.operation: Layer A: 4 deterministic hard gates; Layer B (if A passes): LLM contextual validation + position sizing

modules.name: Executor
modules.output: DRY_RUN log or LIVE order routed via privacy channel
modules.operation: Writes to pipeline_log and trades; in LIVE mode routes via Tor+VPN SOCKS5h channel

modules.name: CBD
modules.input: asset price series {p_a_t}, BTC price series {p_BTC_t} over rolling window W=30
modules.output: composite score Omega_a in [0,1) fed to Analyst context
modules.operation: Convex combination of normalized anomaly magnitude z_tilde and decorrelation score rho_cb

modules.name: EpisodicMemory
modules.operation: SQLite in WAL mode on Docker volume; enables hot restart and cross-episode narrative memory

modules.name: DualChannelExecution
modules.public_channel: Agent -> direct HTTPS -> DEX API (market data retrieval)
modules.private_channel: Agent -> SOCKS5h -> Tor circuit -> VPN -> DEX API (authenticated order placement)
modules.safety_gate: safe = tor_active AND exchange_reachable; blocks execution if false

connections.data: past trade reasoning for same asset

mathematical_spec.name: Instantaneous Return Magnitude
mathematical_spec.description: Per-asset absolute return computed each polling cycle

mathematical_spec.name: Rolling Anomaly Z-Score
mathematical_spec.description: Z-score of return relative to rolling window of W=30 observations

mathematical_spec.name: Pipeline Trigger
mathematical_spec.description: Disjunctive trigger: Z-score gate OR absolute return floor

mathematical_spec.name: Agentic Friction
mathematical_spec.description: Fraction of pipeline invocations that result in non-execution (Risk Manager rejections + Analyst self-abstentions)

mathematical_spec.name: IGP Lock Semaphore
mathematical_spec.latex: L \in \{0,1\}; \text{ admit iff } L=0; L \leftarrow 1 \text{ on admit}; L \leftarrow 0 \text{ on complete}
mathematical_spec.description: Binary mutex controlling pipeline admission; discards concurrent triggers

mathematical_spec.name: BTC Decorrelation Score
mathematical_spec.description: Asset decorrelation from BTC over rolling window W

mathematical_spec.name: Exponential Saturation of Z-Score
mathematical_spec.description: Maps unbounded Z-score to [0,1) to make it commensurable with rho_cb; kappa=0.5

mathematical_spec.name: CBD Composite Score
mathematical_spec.description: Convex combination of normalized anomaly and decorrelation; equal weighting as design choice

mathematical_spec.name: Net PnL with Transaction Costs
mathematical_spec.description: Net profit after round-trip transaction costs

mathematical_spec.name: Round-Trip Cost Decomposition
mathematical_spec.latex: C^{rt}_i = Q_i P_i f_{taker} + \frac{1}{2Q_i}|P_{ask} - P_{bid}| + \lambda \sigma_i \sqrt{\frac{Q_i}{V_i P_i}}
mathematical_spec.description: Exchange fee + half-spread + square-root market impact. Lambda=0.8 calibrated to crypto perpetuals.
mathematical_spec.note: Lambda value and exact fee schedule not fully specified for reproduction; exchange=DEX perpetuals implied

tensor_semantics.name: r_t
tensor_semantics.description: Absolute return per asset per polling cycle

tensor_semantics.name: Omega_a
tensor_semantics.description: CBD composite score per triggered asset, in [0,1)

tensor_semantics.name: analyst_output
tensor_semantics.description: Typed JSON contract from Analyst to Risk Manager

tensor_semantics.name: rm_output
tensor_semantics.description: Typed JSON contract from Risk Manager to Executor

training_pipeline.note: TRAINING-FREE system. No gradient-based training. LLM (qwen3.5:9b) used via inference only. All parameters fixed before experiment and not tuned during session.

datasets.name: Live DEX Perpetual Futures Market
datasets.description: Decentralized perpetual futures exchange with >150 active markets; live data April 6–11 2026
datasets.size: 143,807 volatility samples; 76 assets with at least one trade

metrics.name: Risk Manager rejection rate (of reaching RM)

evaluation_protocol.compute: qwen3.5:9b via remote Ollama instance; containerized microservices on isolated private network
special_conditions: DRY_RUN mode — no real order placement

implementation_assumptions.assumption: LLM: qwen3.5:9b via Ollama REST API at configurable remote endpoint
implementation_assumptions.basis: Explicitly stated in paper; model-agnostic design noted

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Orchestration framework is 'Agno' (custom multi-agent framework wrapping Ollama)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Stated in paper; no open-source link provided

implementation_assumptions.assumption: SQLite in WAL mode for persistence; Docker volume mount for durability
DEX is a crypto perpetual futures exchange (likely Hyperliquid or similar decentralized perp DEX given Tor+VPN routing and OHLCV+L2 data feed)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Inferred from 'decentralized perpetual futures exchange with over 150 active markets'

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Per-asset cooldown of 300s prevents burst re-triggering on same asset
Rolling window W=30 bars at 60s polling = 30 minutes of history in memory
JSON schema validation implemented for all inter-agent contracts; exact schema not published
implementation_assumptions.basis: Implied by 'typed JSON contracts'; specific fields listed for each agent

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Tor SOCKS5h proxy at localhost:9050 (standard Tor daemon port) for private channel
implementation_assumptions.basis: SOCKS5h protocol named; standard Tor configuration assumed

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Exchange API uses authenticated REST endpoints; WebSocket used for real-time L2 orderbook
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard DEX API pattern; OHLCV + L2 data described as inputs

ambiguities.description: Exact DEX exchange not named; cannot reproduce live data feed without knowing the exchange API
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Public DEX perpetuals with REST+WebSocket API; mock data acceptable for dry-run reproduction

ambiguities.description: Only partial system prompt shown; full prompt engineering (including episodic memory briefing format) not fully specified
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Reproduce with documented fields; use reasonable prompt structure

ambiguities.description: ftaker (exact fee schedule) and lambda=0.8 origin not specified; labeled 'calibrated to crypto perpetuals'
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Use 0.05% taker fee (common for DEX perpetuals); lambda=0.8 as stated

ambiguities.description: 'Agno' is described as a custom multi-agent orchestration framework; no repository link or detailed API provided
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Implement equivalent orchestration using asyncio + direct Ollama API calls
